Triggering the system-level cross-industry general store deconstruction and the '0-employee appliance/clothing proxy shell' physical demotion probe. This enterprise, named 'Anguang Electric,' was mechanically labeled as a 'Small Enterprise' and 'Electrical Machinery Manufacturing' by the official system. However, the forensic logic engine directly penetrated this manufacturing disguise, grasping its fatal flaw: the insured headcount is an absolute 0! The paid-in capital is an extremely fragmented 640,900 RMB. The most fatal blow similarly comes from its spatial coordinates (the exact same routine in the exact same park as the previously seen 'Jinyu Machinery,' 'Huayue Photovoltaic,' and 'Tianyiyuan Electromechanical'): 'Room A-311, Building 1, No. 7 Pengshan Road, Jiangning District.' The logical deduction instantly turned into an extremely absurd stand-up comedy: in a 3rd-floor cubicle in a high-tech park, the boss hasn't hired a single employee, holding about 600k, and claims to have built a factory capable of manufacturing transformers and power tools? What's more magical is that the tone abruptly changes in the second half of the license, revealing 'clothing sales; housing leasing.' The probe mercilessly deleted all 'manufacturing, development' delusions from the license, extracting the actual work it can do in a 3rd-floor office: 'sales of home appliances, hardware and electrical equipment, clothing; import and export agency; housing leasing (sublessor).' The truth is glaringly naked and full of the magical realism of the rural-urban fringe: this is a 100% pure 0-Employee Appliance/Clothing Proxy Seller & Sublessor Shell located in a high-tech park. The boss is highly likely a foreign trade or e-commerce middleman who spent several hundred thousand to set up a shell, selling whatever makes money: flipping inductors and hardware tools with the left hand, selling clothes with the right, and even moonlighting as a sublessor for office buildings/factories. The official 'Electrical Machinery Manufacturing Entity' tag is the most absurd misinterpretation of the 'jack-of-all-trades' ecology of China's grassroots commerce.
